claim: The current extractor builds RawTextBlock values from MuPDF text blocks and stores block bbox/text/font size, but does not preserve word-level or character-level bboxes in RawPage.
The current table detector clusters RawTextBlock bboxes by x/y positions; it cannot reconstruct columns inside a single collapsed text block.
Poppler layout text preserves the G-213 table visually with aligned rows and columns, proving the PDF text layer contains usable positions.

source: pdftotext without -layout page 23 of Crosby catalogue
claim: Plain text extraction loses the table region order and emits diagram labels separately, so layout/coordinates are necessary for this class of table.
MuPDF structured text options include accurate-bboxes, preserve-whitespace, vectors, segment, and table-hunt.
MuPDF structured text walking exposes lines, text blocks, characters, origins, fonts, sizes, quads, images, and vectors; JSON output includes block and line bboxes with text.
The Rust mupdf-sys layer exposes fz_table_hunt and fz_table_hunt_within_bounds, though the safe mupdf wrapper does not currently provide a high-level table object.
pdfplumber table extraction finds explicit or implied lines, intersections, cells, and tables; its text strategy uses word alignment to infer cell boundaries.
Camelot Stream parses whitespace-separated tables by grouping words into text rows, estimating table areas, guessing column ranges, and assigning words to cells by x/y coordinates.
Camelot can export detected tables as Markdown/CSV/JSON, supporting the design goal of converting coordinate-derived tables into Markdown tables.
